articles.title: Tether Breakage Across First-generation and Second-generation Vertebral Body Tethering Devices in Treating Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis.
articles.authors: Coleman TA, Kim A, Desai V, Parent S, Miyanji F, Alanay A, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective analysis of prospectively collected multicenter registry
articles.population: Patients with adolescent idiopathic scoliosis treated with thoracic vertebral body tethering
articles.sample_size: 231 patients across 12 centres (179 first-generation, 52 second-generation devices)
articles.outcomes: Radiographic tether breakage rates, revision surgery rates, conversion to posterior spinal fusion rates, and surgeon-observed breakage rates
articles.take_home: Tether breakage occurred in about half of patients with both older and newer vertebral body tethering devices, with no significant difference between the two generations. Whilst surgeons reported seeing fewer breakages with the newer devices, the actual breakage rates on X-rays remained similarly high.

articles.title: Radiographic Measurement of Lower Limb Length Discrepancy: A Comparison of Three Measurement Techniques for Assessing Total and Segmental Limb Lengths.
articles.authors: Kadhim M, Kelly N, Sabharwal S
articles.population: Skeletally immature patients with limb length discrepancy scheduled for epiphysiodesis
articles.outcomes: Comparison of three radiographic measurement methods for total and segmental limb length; measurement differences ranged from 0.5 mm to 1.5 mm between methods; correlation with coronal plane limb alignment
articles.take_home: Three different ways of measuring limb length on X-rays give very similar results, with differences of less than 2 mm. However, it is important to use the same measurement method consistently, particularly in patients with bent legs, as the choice of method can affect the results.

articles.title: Animal-Assisted Therapy Reduces Patient Reported Pain During Office Pin Removal Following Supracondylar Humerus Fracture Fixation: A Randomized Controlled Study.
articles.authors: Arif HA, Feijoo E, Padgett M, Nishikawa A, Williams KA, Strom SF, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 3 to 10 years undergoing pin removal after supracondylar humerus fracture fixation
articles.outcomes: Patient-reported pain using Wong-Baker FACES scale, behavioural distress using FLACC scale, heart rate measurements, and parental-reported anxiety
articles.take_home: Having a certified therapy dog present during pin removal reduced children's pain scores and behavioural distress compared to standard care, with children in the dog group reporting lower pain and showing lower peak heart rates during the procedure.

articles.title: Treatment and outcomes of fractures in children: a registry-based cohort study of 10,144 fractures.
articles.authors: Laaksonen T, Grahn P, Ahonen M, Ahola JA, Helenius I, Nietosvaara Y
articles.population: Children with fractures treated at a Level I trauma centre
articles.outcomes: Surgical timing, treatment methods, complication rates, unplanned reoperations, permanent nerve injuries and deep infections
articles.take_home: Most children with serious arm and leg fractures were operated on quickly and safely, with very few developing nerve damage or infections afterwards. Most children with forearm fractures did not need general anaesthetic and were treated in the emergency department.

articles.title: Postpartum haemorrhage: epidemiology, consequences, and missed opportunities.
articles.authors: Coomarasamy A, Sindhu KN, Gallos I, Periyathambi N, Price MJ, Yunas I, et al.
articles.population: Women giving birth worldwide
articles.sample_size: Estimated 27 million women experiencing PPH annually globally
articles.outcomes: Mortality (43,000 deaths per year), prevalence of PPH (12.6% after vaginal birth and 30.9% after caesarean birth), morbidities including severe anaemia, hysterectomy and organ failure, economic burden (US$10.4 billion annually)
articles.take_home: Postpartum haemorrhage remains the leading cause of maternal death worldwide, occurring in approximately 1 in 8 vaginal births and 1 in 3 caesarean births, but early diagnosis using new WHO definitions and evidence-based treatment bundles, alongside prevention strategies like managing anaemia and avoiding unnecessary caesarean sections, can improve outcomes.

articles.title: Prevention of postpartum haemorrhage: from evidence to implementation at scale.
articles.authors: Gallos ID, Sindhu KN, Yunas I, Sobhy S, Mavrides E, Sheikh L, et al.
articles.study_type: Review and Cochrane network meta-analysis of 122 trials
articles.population: Women giving birth
articles.outcomes: Effectiveness of different uterotonic drugs and combinations for preventing postpartum haemorrhage; side-effect profiles; broader prevention strategies including anaemia management, contraception provision, and management of medical risk factors
articles.take_home: Oxytocin and carbetocin are the most effective single drugs for preventing postpartum haemorrhage with minimal side-effects, though combinations of oxytocin with misoprostol or ergometrine can be considered for high-risk women. Preventing postpartum haemorrhage also requires broader approaches including treating anaemia, providing contraception, managing conditions like gestational diabetes, and ensuring women give birth in properly resourced facilities.

articles.title: Reliability and clinical utility of paediatric ankle fracture classifications : a systematic review.
articles.authors: Teo LS, Xiao L, Yeo BKK, Rayapati R, Lim AKS, Hoipo Hui J, et al.
articles.population: Paediatric ankle fractures
articles.outcomes: Interobserver and intraobserver reliability of paediatric ankle fracture classification systems; clinical utility in guiding treatment decisions; predictive value for premature physeal closure and angular deformity
articles.take_home: The Dias-Tachdjian classification was most reliable for doctors to agree on paediatric ankle fractures, while the Salter-Harris classification was most commonly used and best at predicting early bone growth plate closure. Using both classification types together may help doctors plan treatment more accurately.

articles.title: The impact of Chile's multipronged food labelling and advertising law on early childhood excess weight: a cohort difference-in-differences study.
articles.authors: Paraje G, Valdes N, Macaya AV, Corvalan C, Popkin B
articles.population: School children aged 4-6 years in Chile attending public and publicly subsidised schools
articles.outcomes: Probability of excess weight (overweight or obesity) measured by BMI
articles.take_home: Chile's 2016 food labelling and advertising law, which included warning labels and marketing restrictions, led to measurable reductions in excess weight among young school children, with greater effects after longer exposure to the policy. Girls exposed for 18 months had a 2.85% lower probability of excess weight and boys had a 2.40% lower probability.

articles.title: Low concentration atropine eye drops and progression of myopia in children: multicentre placebo controlled, double masked, randomised trial in the UK (CHAMP-UK).
articles.authors: Azuara-Blanco A, Logan NS, McConnell E, Kearney S, Kirk G, Jones S, et al.
articles.study_type: Multicentre double masked randomised controlled trial
articles.population: Children aged 6-12 years with myopia
articles.sample_size: 289 children (192 atropine, 97 placebo); 230 completed with primary outcome data
articles.outcomes: Low concentration atropine (0.01%) reduced myopia progression by 0.33 dioptres more than placebo and reduced axial length changes by 0.14 mm. Pupil diameter was slightly larger with atropine. No serious adverse events related to the trial drugs.
articles.take_home: Daily atropine eye drops at very low concentration significantly slowed myopia progression in UK children over two years and were well tolerated. The effect was modest but consistent across different ages and severity levels.

articles.title: Exa-cel in Children with Transfusion-Dependent beta-Thalassemia or Sickle Cell Disease.
articles.authors: Frangoul H, de la Fuente J, Chopra Y, Meisel R, Amrolia PJ, Algeri M, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 5 to 11 years with transfusion-dependent beta-thalassemia or sickle cell disease
articles.sample_size: 15 children with beta-thalassemia and 11 with sickle cell disease
articles.outcomes: Transfusion independence for at least 12 consecutive months in beta-thalassemia; freedom from severe vaso-occlusive crises for at least 12 consecutive months in sickle cell disease
articles.take_home: Of children followed for at least 16 months, all those with beta-thalassemia became independent from blood transfusions and all those with sickle cell disease stopped having severe pain crises. However, all children experienced serious side effects, and treatment involves intensive chemotherapy beforehand which caused life-threatening liver damage in some children.

articles.title: Interleukin-10 Autoantibodies and HLA-DRB1*01:03 in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
articles.authors: Gharahdaghi N, Yeh PJ, Ceron-Gutierrez L, Griffin H, Gordon H, Jayamanne C, et al.
articles.population: Patients with inflammatory bowel disease and controls without IBD
articles.outcomes: Detection of interleukin-10 neutralising autoantibodies and association with HLA-DRB1*01:03 genetic marker
articles.take_home: About 3.5% of IBD patients have autoantibodies that block interleukin-10, a protein that normally reduces inflammation, and these patients are much more likely to carry a specific genetic variant called HLA-DRB1*01:03. This finding may help identify a subgroup of IBD patients who might benefit from different treatment approaches.

articles.title: Advances in supporting development in autistic children and youth.
articles.authors: Penner M, Zwaigenbaum L, Piroddi N, Park S, Minhas RS, Singal D
articles.population: Autistic children and youth
articles.outcomes: Developmental trajectories, adaptive skills, co-occurring conditions, autonomy, community participation, intervention benefits and adverse events
articles.take_home: Autistic children benefit from early support for developmental skills, and programmes work better when they include diverse communities and listen to what autistic people say matters most, such as acceptance and meaningful inclusion. Services should also monitor for any harmful effects from therapies.

articles.title: Time is of the Essence! Long-Term Functional, Motor and Sensory Outcomes After Surgical Fixation of Pediatric Medial Epicondyle Fractures: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
articles.authors: Akin AE, Erkan S, Okcu G
articles.population: Children under 18 years old who underwent surgical fixation for medial epicondyle fracture
articles.sample_size: 22 patients with complete long-term follow-up (from 40 initially identified)
articles.outcomes: Functional scores (QuickDASH, VAS), range of motion, grip and pinch strength, sensation, stability, and complications
articles.take_home: Children who had surgery for medial epicondyle fractures had excellent long-term outcomes with good strength, sensation and stability, but those who waited longer before surgery had more restricted elbow movement. Early surgery appears important for maintaining full range of motion.

articles.title: Complications of Implant Removal of Pediatric Femoral Shaft Fractures.
articles.authors: Aslanturk O, Roberts C, Siddique R, Rowland J, Sheffer B, Kelly DM
articles.population: Paediatric patients with femoral shaft fractures treated with intramedullary nails or plates
articles.outcomes: Operative time, estimated blood loss, admission rates, bony overgrowth, wound complications, infection, refracture rate
articles.take_home: Removing plates from healed femur fractures takes longer and causes more blood loss than removing nails, but plates had no documented complications whereas nails had 19 total complications. Refracture after implant removal is very uncommon.

articles.title: Advances in the management of ADHD in children and adolescents.
articles.authors: Robaey P, Rogers MA, Schachar RJ
articles.study_type: Systematic review of ADHD management evidence 2019-2025
articles.population: Children and adolescents with ADHD
articles.outcomes: Diagnostic accuracy of interviews versus rating scales; effectiveness of behavioural parent training, school interventions, and cognitive behavioural therapy; effects of lifestyle changes, cognitive training, and neurofeedback; efficacy of stimulant and non-stimulant medications
articles.take_home: ADHD is a chronic condition that needs ongoing, tailored treatment combining several approaches. Talking to a specialist using semi-structured interviews works better than rating scales alone, whilst behavioural training for parents and school-based support produce small but real improvements, and medications remain an important part of treatment.

articles.title: Factors associated with open reduction of pediatric type III supracondylar humerus fractures.
articles.authors: Najd Mazhar F, Zareie B, Shariatzadeh H, Hasan A, Jafari Kafiabadi M, Akhlaghi N, et al.
articles.population: Children aged 3-13 years with Gartland type III supracondylar humerus fractures
articles.outcomes: Factors associated with need for open reduction and internal fixation versus successful closed reduction
articles.take_home: Older children, those with flexion-type fractures, and those with fractures at lower levels of the bone were significantly more likely to need open surgery rather than closed reduction. Age and fracture location at a lower level were the strongest independent predictors of needing open surgery.

articles.title: A Comparison of the Effectiveness of Different Fixation Strategies in Pediatric and Adolescent Subtrochanteric Femur Fractures.
articles.authors: Munshi MA, Siddabattula R, Najera Saltos D, Pereira D, Cornaghie M, Hosseinzadeh P
articles.population: Children and adolescents aged 18 years or under treated operatively for subtrochanteric femur fractures
articles.outcomes: Radiographic healing, malalignment, pain and mobility scores (PROMIS), and complications
articles.take_home: All three fixation methods (flexible nails, rigid nails, and plating) led to good bone healing and functional improvement in children with subtrochanteric femur fractures. Although flexible nails showed more alignment problems at 6 weeks, these differences were not significant by 3 months, so the choice of fixation should be based on individual patient factors rather than the fracture type alone.

articles.title: Prospective Methodology Results in Higher Rates of Reported Complications Following Pediatric ACL Reconstruction.
articles.authors: Martin H, Ellis H, Agonias K, Van Pelt R, Saper M, Niu EL, et al.
articles.study_type: Prospective multicentre registry study with retrospective comparison
articles.population: Children and young people aged 18 years or under undergoing anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction
articles.sample_size: 8516 cases in the prospective SCORE database
articles.outcomes: Postoperative complications within 8 months including stiffness, reoperation, wound-healing delay, pain, infection, nerve palsy, readmission and deep vein thrombosis
articles.take_home: Prospective tracking of complications after ACL surgery in children found that about 1 in 9 patients experienced a complication within 8 months, with stiffness being the most common. Prospective methods detected more complications like pain, stiffness and reoperation rates than traditional reporting methods.

articles.title: A Comparison of Patient-Reported and Radiographic Outcomes Across Rigid and Flexible Flatfeet Following Calcaneal Lengthening Osteotomy in Pediatric Patients.
articles.authors: Munshi MA, Siddabattula R, Pereira D, Tang N, Hosseinzadeh P
articles.population: Children aged 8 to 18 years with painful flatfeet (rigid or flexible) treated with calcaneal lengthening osteotomy
articles.sample_size: 72 children (114 feet) with minimum 6 months follow-up
articles.outcomes: Patient-reported pain and mobility scores (PROMIS); radiographic measurements including talo-first metatarsal angle, talonavicular uncoverage, incongruency angle, calcaneal pitch, and medial cuneiform-5th metatarsal height
articles.take_home: Calcaneal lengthening surgery significantly improved pain and radiographic measures in children with both flexible and rigid flatfeet, with similar improvements seen in both groups. Both types of flatfoot showed meaningful pain reduction and better alignment on X-rays following surgery.

articles.title: Limb occlusion pressure adjusted tourniquet use results in adequate hemostasis in pediatric limb surgeries: a prospective clinical study.
articles.authors: Jain S, Gupta S, Raghav L, Kumar N, Kumar Y, Kalia P
articles.population: Children aged 1 to 15 years undergoing upper or lower limb surgery
articles.outcomes: Adequacy of hemostasis and tourniquet-related complications
articles.take_home: Using limb occlusion pressure measured by Doppler ultrasound to guide tourniquet pressure worked well in children, with adequate blood control achieved in 95.55% of procedures and no complications observed. This approach allows lower tourniquet pressures to be used compared to standard adult methods.

articles.title: Surgical pinning after loss of reduction in Salter-Harris type I-II distal radius fractures does not improve outcomes: A propensity matched cohort study.
articles.authors: Oral M, Kolac UC, Aydinoglu HS, Ibik S, Morkan M, Sili MV, et al.
articles.population: Children under 16 years with Salter-Harris types I and II distal radius fractures who had loss of reduction after initial closed reduction
articles.outcomes: Mayo wrist score, DASH score, PRWE score, and radiographic parameters including radial height, dorsal angulation, dorsal translation, radial inclination, and physeal alignment
articles.take_home: Children with these fractures who lost their reduction did equally well with continued conservative treatment as with surgery, suggesting surgery may not routinely be needed in children whose bones can still remodel.
